title: Precompute 2D molecule SVGs from SMILES (RDKit at build time)
description: Fetch SMILES for every metabolite (PubChem/ChEBI by xref), cache to data/smiles.json, render solid SVG skeletal structures with RDKit MinimalLib in Node at build time -> web/public/mol/<id>.svg. No dots.

title: Roche-fidelity chart: layout DSL + SVG renderer + molecule structures
description: Replicate the Roche Biochemical Pathways visual language: orthogonal routing, metabolite nodes as name + structure, blue enzyme labels, red cofactor side-arrows, circled +/- regulation, boxed section titles, coordinate grid, white background. Deterministic layout DSL (no force-directed), precomputed molecule SVGs from SMILES, interactive pan/zoom HTML. Screenshot-verify in a loop until zero overlap.

title: CI/CD, security, GitHub Pages deploy
description: Actions: data validation, lint, build, Pages deploy; Dependabot; CodeQL; link check. Public live site.

title: Research swarm — author pathway data in schema
description: Fan out research subagents, each owning a metabolic domain, grounding reactions in KEGG/Reactome/Rhea/UniProt/ChEBI with citations + confidence, writing conflict-free data/pathways/*.json files.

title: Web app scaffold & design system
description: Vite multi-page TypeScript app deployable to GitHub Pages. Hand-built CSS design system (light/dark), shared layout, routing, lean per-page bundles for Lighthouse.

title: Interactive pathway explorer
description: The master interactive chart. Click reactions/enzymes/metabolites, distinct arrow rendering per relationship type, zoom/pan/search/filter, pathway subgraph loading, detail modals.

title: Data pipeline — validate, merge, build master graph
description: Node tooling: validate every data/pathways/*.json against the schema, dedupe entities by stable ID, merge into a single master graph artifact served to the web app, emit search index + glossary. CI-enforced.

title: Schema & data model — the metabolic graph foundation
description: Design the total schema: entities (metabolites, enzymes, genes, complexes, compartments, tissues, pathways), reactions (substrates/products/stoichiometry, EC, reversibility, kinetics, thermodynamics), and a distinct typed arrow for EVERY relationship (substrate, product, catalysis, cofactor, activation, inhibition, allostery, feedback, covalent modification, transport, transcriptional regulation, complex formation, gene-encodes, pathway membership, crosstalk). JSON Schema + TS types + provenance/confidence on every element.

title: Overview LOD: sheet reads as empty + region titles crowd and overrun the frame
description: At overview zoom (k~0.09) met-box/flux strokes (1.5 chart units) render sub-pixel, so the master sheet looks blank with floating red titles. Separately, declutterLabels uses zero-padding collision boxes and never clamps to ir.bounds, so titles visually crowd and 'Catecholamine biosynthesis and degradation' runs past the right frame edge. Fix: hold overview ink at screen width (non-scaling-stroke) and pad + clamp region titles.
close_reason: Overview ink held at screen width, region titles moved to a top layer with knockout halo, collision boxes padded and clamped inside the ruler gutter. Verified by screenshot at overview and detail.

title: Active-site and ligand-binding residue overlay on the 3D structure
description:
In protein.ts fetch UniProt features at runtime (rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/{acc}.json -> ACT_SITE, BINDING, SITE, METAL, MOD_RES, CA_BIND) and render those residues as sticks/spheres colored by role on the AlphaFold model in 3Dmol, with a side-panel list (residue, role, bound ligand/ion) and a zoom-to-site button. Turns a generic ribbon into a mechanistic, binding-aware picture from authoritative, grounded data.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=protein; effort=M; impact=high]

title: External probe/reagent cross-links from every node
description:
Extend xrefUrl and the drawers with links to ChEMBL, BindingDB, Guide to Pharmacology, the Chemical Probes Portal, Probes & Drugs, and PubChem BioAssay, resolved by inchikey (metabolites) or uniprot (enzymes). Near-zero cost, high daily utility: jump from any node straight to probe availability, selectivity, and potency without leaving your train of thought.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=S; impact=medium]

title: Color reactions by thermodynamic favorability (surface deltaG'°)
description:
The schema already has deltaGPrimeKjPerMol but nothing renders it. Color reaction nodes/edges on a diverging exergonic->endergonic scale, badge irreversible/rate-limiting committed steps, and draw a cumulative delta-G'° profile chart along pathwayStep. Backfill missing values from eQuilibrator component-contribution data at build time. Explains directionality and why control points sit where they do; the field exists and just needs to become visible.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=S; impact=high]

title: Mass- and charge-balance validator with a 'balanced' badge
description:
Extend tools/validate.mjs to sum substrate vs product elemental formulas and charges (weighted by stoichiometry) per reaction and assert conservation; emit a report plus a balanced check/warn badge in the reaction drawer. Fail CI on unbalanced high-confidence reactions. Enforces non-negotiable chemistry, catches curation errors mechanically, and is a strong credibility signal for a citation-grounded atlas.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=data-schema; effort=S; impact=high]
close_reason: Validator implemented in tools/lib/balance.mjs + validate.mjs (warning-level) with tests; fix-swarm now correcting flagged data

title: Activate the dormant gene layer: auto-emit gene-encodes edges
description:
In tools/build-graph.mjs, for every enzyme carrying a gene symbol (or xrefs.ensembl), synthesize a Gene node and a gene-encodes edge gene->enzyme. Today genes only come from an empty mod.genes array so the gene-encodes arrow never renders. Namespace ids by Ensembl. Add a 'Genes' toggle to the explorer. Near-free change that unlocks all downstream gene-expression/regulation features.

[from expert ideation — Molecular & cell biologist; category=data-schema; effort=S; impact=high]
close_reason: Implemented: build-graph synthesizes gene nodes + gene-encodes edges

title: Render a 2D structure for every metabolite from SMILES/InChIKey
description:
Add a StructureThumb component that renders metabolite.smiles to an SVG depiction client-side via RDKit MinimalLib WASM (lazy-loaded) or SmilesDrawer. Backfill missing SMILES from PubChem PUG-REST by inchikey at build time. Show it in glossary cards, the explorer drawer header, and as an optional Cytoscape node background-image (data-URI SVG) toggle for metabolite nodes. This is the single biggest missing primitive: a chemistry atlas showing only 'C6H13O9P' is unreadable.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=high]

title: Author BCAA metabolism pathway module
description: data/pathways/branched-chain-amino-acid-metabolism.json — grounded in KEGG map00280, UniProt, Lehninger 8e

title: Agent docs & skills
description: Update CLAUDE.md, write subagent skills for schema authoring, data validation, and UI conventions so every agent works consistently.

title: Three.js + GSAP animation system
description: Reusable biology-themed Three.js/GSAP animation modules used across the site, respecting reduced-motion + performance budgets.

title: 700 expert ideas & incorporation
description: 100 ideas each from structural biologist, chemical biologist, biochemist, mol/cell biologist, organic chemist, ML scientist, UI/UX designer. Score and implement the good ones.

title: Performance & Lighthouse 100
description: WebGPU/WASM/Rust acceleration where it helps; Lighthouse feedback loop to perfect scores across categories.

title: Educational content — learn, glossary, about
description: Glossary lookup page, 'what are metabolic pathways' explainers, learning links across the metabolic system, about pages.

title: Protein structure viewer (AlphaFold/PDB)
description: Lazy-loaded 3D protein viewer fetching AlphaFold (by UniProt) and PDB structures, wired to enzyme nodes in the chart.

title: ~50 compounds still describe themselves inconsistently across modules
description: The new cross-module identity gate in tools/validate.mjs reports 53 compounds with conflicting formula/charge between modules; ATP, AMP and NAD+ are fixed. The rest are mostly (a) missing 'charge' fields (e.g. water/H+/O2 in modules that omitted it) and (b) stale neutral formulas paired with anion charges in modules that were not in the 20-module balance-fix set (e.g. ADP C10H15N5O10P2/-3 in oxidative-phosphorylation, purine-de-novo-synthesis). Fix by converging each ChEBI id onto its authoritative microspecies formula+charge (the ChEBI compound API returns both). Note: edit format-preserving — files use two different JSON styles and a naive JSON.stringify rewrite creates a 15k-line reformat diff.

title: Formula/charge convention mismatch in several metabolites
description: Adversarial review flagged 4 modules (glutamate-glutamine, fatty-acid-synthesis, gluconeogenesis, warburg-effect-cancer): metabolite 'formula' is the neutral protonated acid (e.g. ATP C10H16N5O13P3) while 'charge' is the physiological anion (e.g. -4). Not fabrication — identifiers/reactions are correct — but the formula should match the charged species (or charge set to 0). Best fixed by implementing the mass/charge-balance validator (see ideation backlog) which catches these mechanically across all modules.
notes: Full scope: 58 mass-imbalanced + 21 charge-imbalanced reactions across 20 modules. Balance-fix swarm dispatched to correct formulas/charges + add H+/H2O until validator is clean.
close_reason: Fixed across 20 modules: charge imbalances 21->0, mass 58->3 (3 documented flavoprotein exceptions). Validator gate + drawer badge added.

title: Known inhibitors and chemical probes per enzyme (ChEMBL by UniProt)
description:
Query ChEMBL (EBI, CORS-enabled) by the enzyme's uniprot accession: /mechanism for MoA drugs and /activity filtered to pChEMBL >= 6 for potent bioactives. Show a 'Probes & inhibitors' table in the enzyme drawer with a SMILES thumbnail, standard type (IC50/Ki/Kd), pChEMBL, action type, and ChEMBL link. Cache into a build-time JSON to avoid live calls. Maps every enzyme to the molecules that perturb it and grounds the inhibition arrow types in real chemical matter.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=L; impact=high]

title: Highlight the reacting center: 'what bond changes' per reaction
description:
With RDKit-WASM compute the maximum common substructure between summed substrates and products, then highlight the atoms/bonds NOT in the MCS (the reacting region) in aligned 2D depictions, and label the transferred group (phosphoryl, hydride, methyl, acyl, CO2, H2O). Store an optional curated reactingAtoms[] override for cases MCS gets wrong. Turns each arrow from a label into a mechanistic diagram, the literal chemical logic of the reaction.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=L; impact=high]

title: SMARTS / substructure search across all metabolites
description:
Client-side RDKit substructure matcher with a query bar (type SMARTS or pick preset chemotype buttons: phosphate, thiol, catechol, Michael acceptor, carboxylate, primary amine, thioester). Highlight matching atoms in thumbnails and filter the explorer/glossary to hits. Lets a chemical biologist ask structural questions across all of metabolism: where are the electrophiles, free thiols, acyl-phosphates.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=ml-search; effort=M; impact=high]

title: Transcription-factor to target regulon edges from TRRUST/CollecTRI
description:
The transcriptional-activation/repression arrows exist but nothing populates them. Bundle TRRUST v2 or CollecTRI (TF, target, mode, confidence), filter to genes present in the atlas, and emit signed TF->gene regulation edges with confidence stored in provenance. Expose as a 'Transcriptional regulation' layer. Small flat files that filter cleanly to the modeled gene set.

[from expert ideation — Molecular & cell biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=high]

title: Tissue-specific isozyme families with per-isozyme expression + isozyme edges
description:
Model canonical isozyme sets (HK1/2/3 vs GCK, PKM1/PKM2/PKLR, LDHA/LDHB, PFKM/PFKL/PFKP, ALDOA/B/C) as distinct enzyme nodes linked by isozyme edges, each with its dominant tissue and distinguishing kinetics (e.g. GCK high Km, no G6P product inhibition). Add an 'isozyme switch' that highlights the dominant paralog for a chosen tissue. Isozyme substitution is core physiological logic the flat Roche chart never captured.

[from expert ideation — Molecular & cell biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=high]

title: Curated cofactor-mechanism catalog
description:
Author data/cofactors.json classifying each coenzyme by the chemistry it enables: NAD(P)+ hydride/2e-, FAD/FMN 1e- or 2e-, PLP transamination/decarboxylation/racemization, TPP acyl-anion umpolung, biotin CO2 carrier, SAM methyl donor, CoA thioester carrier, THF one-carbon, lipoate, ascorbate. Each entry: enabled reaction classes, electron count, key functional group, canonical example. Link reaction.cofactors[] chips to these cards. Connects the existing cofactors field to real mechanism.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=high]

title: Physicochemical property panel for metabolites
description:
From SMILES compute client-side (RDKit) MW, cLogP, TPSA, H-bond donors/acceptors, rotatable bonds, aromatic ring count, formal charge, and Lipinski Ro5 flags (or fetch from PubChem by inchikey). Render as a compact table plus a small radar in the metabolite drawer with 'high-energy'/'polar'/'membrane-permeant' heuristic badges. LogP, TPSA, and charge govern permeability, compartmentation, reactivity, and druglikeness.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=high]

title: Bundle HPA + GTEx tissue expression per gene with an in-drawer sparkline
description:
Add an expression array to gene/enzyme {tissue, nTPM, source}. At build time join HPA rna_tissue_consensus.tsv and/or GTEx median-TPM (keyed on Ensembl) filtered to atlas genes into the graph. In openDrawer() render an inline SVG bar-strip (no external lib) of nTPM across ~30 tissues, log-scaled, with the top tissue labeled. HPA blocks CORS so bundle rather than fetch at runtime, keeping it fully static.

[from expert ideation — Molecular & cell biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=high]

title: Extract and display co-crystallized ligands (HETATM) from PDB structures
description:
Parse HETATM records from the PDB files already loaded in protein.ts; list non-water heteroatoms by their 3-letter chem-component IDs; add a 'show ligands' representation (sticks + optional pocket surface) with zoom-to-ligand, and link each ligand to its RCSB ligand page and PubChem entry. Bound substrates, analogs, inhibitors, and cofactors are the most direct binding-mode evidence available and the viewer currently discards them.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=protein; effort=S; impact=high]

title: Perpetual lifecycle — issue hunting & auto-fix
description: Scaffolding for continuous issue discovery (file GitHub issues) and a maintenance loop that sweeps open issues and ships fixes.

title: Auth & accounts
description: Client-side account/login scaffold (clearly labeled demo on static hosting) with saved views/bookmarks; architecture ready for a real backend.

title: Minor review findings: ACC2 residue, insulin kind, Warburg GLUD1 route
description: From adversarial swarm review (non-blocking): (1) fatty-acid-synthesis reg_ampk_acc uses ACC2 Ser212 (rodent); human ACACB is Ser221. (2) glutamate-glutamine + warburg store insulin as a metabolite; a hormone/protein kind is more accurate. (3) warburg war11 models glutamate->aKG via GLUD1 only; in most tumors the dominant anaplerotic route is transaminase (GOT2/GPT2) — consider adding. All honestly disclosed in module notes.

title: Curated stepwise 'arrow-pushing' mechanism cards
description:
Extend the schema with an optional mechanism.steps[] (description, movingGroup, bondsFormed[], bondsBroken[], catalyticResidue) and author it for landmark reactions: aldolase Schiff base / retro-aldol, GAPDH thiohemiacetal + acyl-phosphate, PLP transamination, enolase Mg2+ E1cb. Render as an ordered mechanism card with optional inline SVG. Curly-arrow mechanism is the essence of chemical logic and what would make Metabolian a genuine successor rather than a re-skin; high effort and curation-heavy, so it trails the automated wins.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=L; impact=high]

title: Isotope / atom-fate tracer for central carbon metabolism
description:
Curate atom mapping for the core pathways and let the user pick a labeled position (glucose C1 vs C6) and watch its fate to CO2, lactate, or acetyl-CoA, highlighting the traced atom through the 2D depictions and the graph path. 13C tracing is how metabolic flux is measured in the lab; central metabolism is small enough to curate exactly, but the atom-mapping curation makes it a large late-stage effort.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=education; effort=L; impact=medium]

title: Binding-pocket surface physicochemistry viewer
description:
In 3Dmol render the molecular surface around UniProt/M-CSA-annotated binding residues colored by residue hydrophobicity (Kyte-Doolittle) and charged-residue class (Asp/Glu vs Lys/Arg/His), so the user can read whether the pocket is hydrophobic, polar, or charged, with no docking required. Pocket character is what a chemist reads first when reasoning about what could bind.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=protein; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Structure-derived binding-affinity table (RCSB GraphQL)
description:
For each enzyme pull curated Ki/Kd/IC50 annotations from RCSB PDB structure records (BindingDB/PDBBind fields via the data.rcsb.org GraphQL API) and present a sortable affinity table (ligand, value, assay type, PDB id, resolution) that deep-links each row to the 3D viewer preloaded with that holo structure. Quantitative affinity tied to a solved complex is the gold currency of binding.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=protein; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Dominant microspecies / protonation state at physiological pH
description:
Display the physiologically dominant ionization state at pH 7.3 using the curated charge field plus an optional pKa[] list and a Henderson-Hasselbalch fraction, and flag when the drawn neutral SMILES differs from the real in-cell microspecies (citrate3-, ATP4-). Metabolites are overwhelmingly ionized at cytosolic pH; the true microspecies sharpens reasoning about charge, transport, chelation, and binding electrostatics.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: PTM / covalent-modification chemistry catalog
description:
For the covalent arrow types (phosphorylation, acetylation, methylation, ubiquitination, ADP-ribosylation) attach the specific chemistry: target residue, added group, monoisotopic mass shift (phospho +79.966, acetyl +42.011, methyl +14.016), and the reversing 'eraser' enzyme. Render on covalent-modification edges and in the enzyme drawer. Ties PTMs directly to proteomics readouts and PTM-reader probe strategies.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist, Molecular & cell biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Electrophile / covalent-warhead flags (probe relevance)
description:
SMARTS-based tagging of electrophilic warheads and Michael acceptors among metabolites and curated probes (acrylamide, chloroacetamide, aldehyde, alpha-beta-unsaturated carbonyl, epoxide), with a note of the likely nucleophilic target (catalytic Cys/Ser/Lys). Covalent and activity-based probes are central to modern chemical biology; this flags where covalent chemistry is plausible.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=S; impact=medium]

title: Chemical-similarity and scaffold neighbor view
description:
Precompute Morgan fingerprints and pairwise Tanimoto (RDKit, build-time) across metabolites; in the drawer show 'structurally similar metabolites' grouped by Bemis-Murcko scaffold. Optionally embed a precomputed 2D chemical-space scatter (PCA/UMAP) as its own view. Reveals substrate-analog relationships and why enzymes cross-react or an inhibitor mimics a substrate.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=ml-search; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Group-transfer potential ladder and high-energy intermediate flags
description:
Add a highEnergy boolean and phosphorylTransferPotentialKjPerMol to metabolites (PEP, 1,3-BPG, acetyl-CoA, ATP, creatine-phosphate); render a ranked phosphoryl-transfer ladder and badge high-energy intermediates in reaction views. Explains substrate-level phosphorylation and why PEP->ATP and 1,3-BPG->ATP are favorable.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=S; impact=medium]

title: Carbon oxidation-state tracking per reaction
description:
Algorithmically compute the mean carbon oxidation state from structure (RDKit) for substrate vs product, then auto-label each reaction as oxidation/reduction/redox-neutral with the electron count, and annotate which carbon changed. Oxidation-state change is the rigorous definition of 'is this an oxidation?', sharpening the chemical logic beyond enzyme naming and revealing hidden redox steps.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Reduction-potential (redox) tower
description:
Add standardReductionPotentialV to cofactor couples and half-reactions; render a vertical E'° tower (NAD+/NADH -0.32 V to O2/H2O +0.82 V) with electron-carrier arrows, and compute delta-E'° -> delta-G for redox reactions. Complements the existing electron-transfer arrow type and the oxidative-phosphorylation module. Makes 'electrons flow downhill' quantitative and visual.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Cofactor and metal usage dashboard + explorer facet
description:
Aggregate across the merged graph which reactions consume NAD(P)H, ATP/GTP, PLP, SAM, CoA, biotin, THF, or metal ions (from reaction.cofactors[] and enzyme.cofactors[]) and add an explorer facet like 'show all PLP-dependent reactions' plus a sortable dashboard. Surfaces mechanistic families that cut across pathways, a cross-cutting lens the flat chart could never provide.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=explorer-ux; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: pLDDT confidence and PAE overlay in the protein viewer
description:
Color AlphaFold models by pLDDT read from the B-factor column (very-high/confident/low bands) and render the PAE JSON (alphafold.ebi.ac.uk predicted-aligned-error file) as a heatmap. Warn when an annotated binding/active site falls in a low-confidence or disordered region. You cannot trust a mechanism claim placed on a low-pLDDT loop; making reliability explicit is basic scientific hygiene.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=protein; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Reaction SMILES and atom-mapped depiction from Rhea
description:
Store reactionSmiles (from the Rhea id already in xrefs) and render the balanced reaction as aligned reactant->product 2D structures using Rhea's atom mapping where available, falling back to the equation string. Feeds the reacting-center highlighter. Rhea is already an accepted provenance source; machine-readable reaction SMILES make each transformation depictable and balance-checkable.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Precomputed functional-group / reactive-moiety tagging
description:
At build time tag each metabolite with a chemotypes[] array via an RDKit SMARTS library (aldehyde, ketone, acyl-phosphate, thioester, enol phosphate, Michael acceptor, epoxide, disulfide, phosphoanhydride). Expose as explorer/glossary facets and badge reactive groups on nodes. Reactive moieties are where mechanism and covalent-probe chemistry happen, making mechanistic families filterable.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Actual-ΔG / mass-action displacement calculator
description:
Interactive panel where the user sets metabolite concentrations (or picks a tissue preset) and the app computes delta-G = delta-G'° + RT*ln(Q) and the disequilibrium ratio Q/K', flagging near-equilibrium vs far-from-equilibrium steps. Pure client-side arithmetic over reaction stoichiometry. Teaches that flux control sits at the far-from-equilibrium reactions.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=education; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Michaelis-Menten and inhibition simulator from existing kinetics
description:
Use the schema's kinetics[] (kmMilliMolar, kcatPerSec, vmax) to draw an interactive v-vs-[S] curve; overlay competitive/noncompetitive inhibition given a user Ki; plot two isozymes on the same axes (low-Km hexokinase vs high-Km glucokinase) to show substrate specialization. Makes Km, saturation, and isozyme division-of-labor tangible from data already captured but invisible.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=education; effort=M; impact=medium]

title: Catalytic-efficiency 'enzyme scorecard' (kcat/Km)
description:
Derive the specificity constant kcat/Km from the existing kinetics[] fields, compare to the diffusion limit (~1e8-1e9 M^-1 s^-1), badge 'catalytically perfect' enzymes (e.g. triosephosphate isomerase), and tabulate per enzyme with source organism. The single most informative kinetic number, nearly free to compute from data the schema already holds.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=S; impact=medium]

title: Enzyme catalytic-mechanism cards from M-CSA
description:
Build-time ingest of EBI's Mechanism and Catalytic Site Atlas (freely downloadable, keyed by UniProt/EC): store catalytic residues with roles (nucleophile, general acid/base, electrostatic stabilizer) and the prose mechanism. Render in the enzyme drawer and map residues onto the 3D viewer, composing with the UniProt overlay. M-CSA is the authoritative curated source for how enzymes actually work at the residue/electron level.

[from expert ideation — Chemical biologist; category=science-content; effort=L; impact=high]

title: Author a master metabolic transcription-factor module
description:
New data/pathways/metabolic-tfs.json modeling SREBP-1c/2, ChREBP, PPAR-alpha/gamma/delta, LXR, HIF-1-alpha, NRF2, PGC-1-alpha, FOXO1 and the CREB/ATF axis as regulator entities with transcriptional edges to metabolic target genes (SREBP2->HMGCR/LDLR; HIF-1-alpha->glycolytic genes; PPAR-alpha->beta-oxidation), each with PMID provenance. Turns the atlas from 'enzymes' into 'why enzyme levels change,' the crux of nutritional/physiological interpretation.

[from expert ideation — Molecular & cell biologist; category=science-content; effort=L; impact=high]
